content: We can reframe agent memory as an iterative self-refining system rather than a static storage or retrieval layer: an agent can pull its own past memory traces as initial outputs, then use self-feedback to prune irrelevant memories, correct misattributed past experiences, and restructure associative links between connected memories through repeated Self-Refine iterations, all without requiring supervised human annotation of memory organization. This creates a dynamically evolving agent memory that aligns with how human long-term memory updates through repeated reflection.
